metadata.display_name: Kling Video v3 Image to Video [Pro]
metadata.description: Kling 3.0 Pro: Top-tier image-to-video with cinematic visuals, fluid motion, and native audio generation, with custom element support.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/kling-video/v3/pro/image-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/kling-video/v3/pro/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Kling V3 (pro) Image to Video API.

The Kling V3 model is the latest generation of image-to-video with
improved visual quality and motion consistency. This version also
supports native audio generation when the `generate_audio` parameter is enabled.

multi_prompt.description: List of prompts for multi-shot video generation. If provided, divides the video into multiple shots.
multi_prompt.title: Multi Prompt

cfg_scale.description:
The CFG (Classifier Free Guidance) scale is a measure of how close you want
            the model to stick to your prompt.
cfg_scale.title: Cfg Scale

negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.default: blur, distort, and low quality

shot_type.description: The type of multi-shot video generation. 'intelligent' lets the model automatically determine shot structure.
shot_type.title: Shot Type

start_image_url.description: URL of the image to be used for the video
start_image_url.title: Start Image Url
start_image_url.limit_description: Max file size: 10.0MB, Min width: 300px, Min height: 300px, Min aspect ratio: 0.40, Max aspect ratio: 2.50, Timeout: 20.0s

prompt.description: Text prompt for video generation. Either prompt or multi_prompt must be provided, but not both.
prompt.title: Prompt
examples: The craftsman slowly examines the bowl, turning it gently in his weathered hands. His eyes reflect years of wisdom. Subtle smile forms on his face. Dust particles drift in warm light. Breathing motion, blinking eyes.

generate_audio.description: Whether to generate native audio for the video. Supports Chinese and English voice output. Other languages are automatically translated to English. For English speech, use lowercase letters; for acronyms or proper nouns, use uppercase.
generate_audio.title: Generate Audio

duration.description: The duration of the generated video in seconds
duration.title: Duration

anyOf.limit_description: Max file size: 10.0MB, Min width: 300px, Min height: 300px, Min aspect ratio: 0.40, Max aspect ratio: 2.50, Timeout: 20.0s

end_image_url.description: URL of the image to be used for the end of the video
end_image_url.title: End Image Url

elements.description: Elements (characters/objects) to include in the video. Each example can either be an image set (frontal + reference images) or a video. Reference in prompt as @Element1, @Element2, etc.
elements.title: Elements

prompt.description: The prompt for this shot.
prompt.title: Prompt

duration.description: The duration of this shot in seconds
duration.title: Duration

anyOf.limit_description: Max file size: 200.0MB, Min width: 720px, Min height: 720px, Max width: 2160px, Max height: 2160px, Min duration: 3.0s, Max duration: 10.05s, Min FPS: 24.0, Max FPS: 60.0, Timeout: 30.0s

video_url.description: The video URL of the element. A request can only have one element with a video.
video_url.title: Video Url

items.limit_description: Max file size: 10.0MB, Min width: 300px, Min height: 300px, Min aspect ratio: 0.40, Max aspect ratio: 2.50, Timeout: 20.0s

reference_image_urls.description: Additional reference images from different angles. 1-3 images supported. At least one image is required.
reference_image_urls.title: Reference Image Urls

anyOf.limit_description: Max file size: 10.0MB, Min width: 300px, Min height: 300px, Min aspect ratio: 0.40, Max aspect ratio: 2.50, Timeout: 20.0s

frontal_image_url.description: The frontal image of the element (main view).
frontal_image_url.title: Frontal Image Url

voice_id.description: The voice ID for this element. The voice will be binded to the element and references to this element will use the binded voice. Voice binding is only supported for video elements, and cannot be used with image elements. Get voice IDs from the following endpoint: https://fal.ai/models/fal-ai/kling-video/create-voice
voice_id.title: Voice Id

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: ByteDance's most advanced image-to-video model. Animate still images into cinematic video with synchronized audio, start and end frame control, and motion prompts.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for bytedance/seedance-2.0/image-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the bytedance/seedance-2.0/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about: Image To Video

generate_audio.description: Whether to generate synchronized audio for the video, including sound effects, ambient sounds, and lip-synced speech. The cost of video generation is the same regardless of whether audio is generated or not.
generate_audio.title: Generate Audio

resolution.description: Video resolution - 480p for faster generation, 720p for balance, 1080p for highest quality.
resolution.title: Resolution

aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio of the generated video. Use 16:9 for landscape, 9:16 for portrait/vertical, 1:1 for square, 21:9 for ultrawide cinematic, or auto to infer from the input image.

end_image_url.description: The URL of the image to use as the last frame of the video. When provided, the generated video will transition from the starting image to this ending image. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max 30 MB.
end_image_url.title: End Image Url

image_url.description: The URL of the starting frame image to animate. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max 30 MB.
image_url.title: Image Url

end_user_id.description: The unique user ID of the end user.
end_user_id.title: End User Id

seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. Note that results may still vary slightly even with the same seed.
seed.title: Seed

duration.description: Duration of the video in seconds. Supports 4 to 15 seconds, or auto to let the model decide based on the prompt.
duration.title: Duration

prompt.description: The text prompt describing the desired motion and action for the video.
examples: An octopus finds a football in the ocean and excitedly calls its octopus friends to come and play. Cut scene to an octopus football game under the sea.
prompt.title: Prompt

seed.description: The seed used for generation.
seed.title: Seed

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.
url.title: Url

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

audio_urls.description: Reference audio to guide video generation. Refer to them in the prompt as @Audio1, @Audio2, etc. Supported formats: MP3, WAV. metadata.description: Generate videos from your image prompts using Veo 3.1 fast.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/veo3.1/fast/image-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo3.1/fast/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos by animating an input image using Google's Veo 3.1 Fast model.

The prompt should describe how to animate the input image. Include:
- Action: How the image should be animated
- Style: Desired animation style
- Camera motion (optional): How camera should move
- Ambiance (optional): Desired mood and atmosphere

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio
- Natural motion and realistic animations
- Control over animation via text prompts

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

auto_fix.title: Auto Fix
auto_fix.description: Whether to automatically attempt to fix prompts that fail content policy or other validation checks by rewriting them.

generate_audio.title: Generate Audio
generate_audio.description: Whether to generate audio for the video.

safety_tolerance.title: Safety Tolerance
safety_tolerance.description: The safety tolerance level for content moderation. 1 is the most strict (blocks most content), 6 is the least strict.

image_url.title: Image URL
image_url.description: URL of the input image to animate. Should be 720p or higher resolution in 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio. If the image is not in 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio, it will be cropped to fit.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The text prompt describing the video you want to generate
examples:
A monkey and polar bear host a casual podcast about AI inference, bringing their unique perspectives from different environments (tropical vs. arctic) to discuss how AI systems make decisions and process information.
Sample Dialogue:
Monkey (Banana): "Welcome back to Bananas & Ice! I am Banana"
Polar Bear (Ice): "And I'm Ice!"

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: The duration of the generated video.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio of the generated video. Only 16:9 and 9:16 are supported.

seed.description: The seed for the random number generator.
seed.title: Seed

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video.

negative_prompt.description: A negative prompt to guide the video generation.
negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
file_size.title: File Size

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.
file_name.title: File Name

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.
content_type.title: Content Type

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

video_urls.description: Reference videos to guide video generation. Refer to them in the prompt as @Video1, @Video2, etc. Supported formats: MP4, MOV. audio_urls.description: Reference audio to guide video generation. Refer to them in the prompt as @Audio1, @Audio2, etc. Supported formats: MP3, WAV. x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos by animating an input image using Google's Veo 3.1 Lite model.

A lightweight, cost-effective variant of Veo 3.1.

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- 4, 6, or 8 second duration (1080p requires 8s)
- 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio
- Audio on or off (silent video)

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

metadata.description: Cosmos3 is a collection of Omnimodal world models capable of generating dynamic, high-quality video, image, audio, and action commands from combinations of text, image, video, and action trajectory inputs.

x-fal-metadata.about: Generate a video from a first-frame image and prompt using Cosmos3-Super via vLLM-Omni.

agentic_max_iterations.description: Maximum agentic prompt stages when agentic generation is enabled.
agentic_max_iterations.title: Agentic Max Iterations

guidance_scale.description: Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values increase prompt adherence at the cost of diversity.
guidance_scale.title: Guidance scale (CFG)

sync_mode.title: Sync Mode
sync_mode.description: If `True`, the video is returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

examples: The camera slowly pushes in as the subject turns their head toward the light, hair drifting in a gentle breeze, dust motes floating through warm afternoon sun.
prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Text prompt describing the motion and scene of the video to generate.

agentic_early_stop.title: Agentic Early Stop
agentic_early_stop.description: Stop the agentic loop early when the critic score clears the strict quality threshold.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: The same seed and prompt given to the same model version will produce the same video every time.

enable_agentic_generation.title: Enable Agentic Generation
enable_agentic_generation.description: Enable the iterative Cosmos agentic loop: prompt upsampling, candidate video generation, VLM critique of sampled frames, and prompt rewrite. Each candidate is a full render, so this is substantially slower and costlier than a single generation.

num_inference_steps.description: Number of denoising steps. More steps yield higher quality but take longer.
num_inference_steps.title: Num Inference Steps

agentic_samples_per_iteration.description: Candidate videos to generate and judge per agentic iteration. The best candidate advances to the next rewrite stage.
agentic_samples_per_iteration.title: Agentic Samples Per Iteration

examples: The video captures a series of frames showing macroblocking artifacts, chromatic aberration, high-frequency noise, and rolling shutter distortion. It includes static with no motion, motion blur, over-saturation, shaky footage, low resolution, grainy texture, pixelated images, poorly lit areas, underexposed and overexposed scenes, poor color balance, washed out colors, choppy sequences, jerky movements, low frame rate, bit-depth compression artifacts, color banding, unnatural transitions, outdated special effects, fake elements, unconvincing visuals, poorly edited content, jump cuts, hard cut, visual noise, and flickering. It features moiré patterns, edge halos, and temporal aliasing. Furthermore, the content defies common sense, generating illogical scenarios, nonsensical entities, absurd character behaviors, and conceptual paradoxes that violate basic human reasoning and everyday reality. The video looks like a surreal or glitchy hallucination. Overall, the video is of poor quality.
negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.default: The video captures a series of frames showing macroblocking artifacts, chromatic aberration, high-frequency noise, and rolling shutter distortion. It includes static with no motion, motion blur, over-saturation, shaky footage, low resolution, grainy texture, pixelated images, poorly lit areas, underexposed and overexposed scenes, poor color balance, washed out colors, choppy sequences, jerky movements, low frame rate, bit-depth compression artifacts, color banding, unnatural transitions, outdated special effects, fake elements, unconvincing visuals, poorly edited content, jump cuts, hard cut, visual noise, and flickering. It features moiré patterns, edge halos, and temporal aliasing. Furthermore, the content defies common sense, generating illogical scenarios, nonsensical entities, absurd character behaviors, and conceptual paradoxes that violate basic human reasoning and everyday reality. The video looks like a surreal or glitchy hallucination. Overall, the video is of poor quality.
negative_prompt.description: Content to steer the generation away from (artifacts, unwanted motion). Defaults to NVIDIA's recommended i2v negative prompt; pass an empty string to disable.

image_size.title: Image Size
image_size.description: The size of the generated video. The request is clamped and snapped to the nearest supported NVIDIA tier (256p/480p/720p) and aspect ratio.

enable_prompt_expansion.title: Enable Prompt Expansion
enable_prompt_expansion.description: If true, the Cosmos3-Nano Reasoner (a VLM that sees the first frame) rewrites the prompt into the dense caption Cosmos3 was trained on. The app starts a local Reasoner by default, or uses COSMOS_PROMPT_UPSAMPLER_BASE_URL when configured. Falls back to the raw prompt if expansion fails.

examples: Front-facing 'invisible' action-cam on a skydiver in freefall above bright clouds; camera locked on his face. He speaks over the wind with clear lipsync: 'This is insanely fun! You've got to try it—book a tandem and go!' Natural wind roar, voice close-mic'd and slightly compressed so it's intelligible. Midday sun, goggles and jumpsuit flutter, altimeter visible, parachute rig on shoulders. Energetic but stable framing with subtle shake; brief horizon roll. End on first tug of canopy and wind noise dropping.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate video from image or video input using WAN 2.7 I2V model.

Scenarios:
- First-frame-to-video: provide image_url
- First+last-frame-to-video: provide image_url + end_image_url
- Video continuation: provide video_url
- Any scenario can include driving audio via audio_url

metadata.description: Generate videos from a first and last framed using Google's Veo 3.1

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/veo3.1/first-last-frame-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo3.1/first-last-frame-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos by animating between a first and last frame using Google's Veo 3.1 model.

The prompt should describe how to animate between the first and last frame. Include:
- Action: How the first and last frame should be animated
- Style: Desired animation style
- Camera motion (optional): How camera should move
- Ambiance (optional): Desired mood and atmosphere

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio
- Natural motion and realistic animations
- Control over animation via text prompts

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

metadata.description: Generate Videos from images using Google's Veo 3.1

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/veo3.1/reference-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo3.1/reference-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos from reference image(s) and text using Google's Veo 3.1 model.

The prompt should describe how to animate between the first and last frame. Include:
- Action: How the first and last frame should be animated
- Style: Desired animation style
- Camera motion (optional): How camera should move
- Ambiance (optional): Desired mood and atmosphere

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- Natural motion and realistic animations
- Control over animation via text prompts

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/wan-25-preview/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate a video from an image using WAN 2.5 image-to-video model.

WAN 2.5 image-to-video model features:
- Using an image as the first frame
- Motion generation based on text prompt
- 480p and 1080p resolutions
- 5 or 10-second video generation
- Processing time: 1-3 minutes (longer for 10s videos)

Image requirements:
- Formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG (no alpha), BMP, WEBP
- Resolution: 360-2000 pixels (width and height)
- Max size: 10MB

enable_safety_checker.description: If set to true, the safety checker will be enabled.
enable_safety_checker.title: Enable Safety Checker

negative_prompt.description: Negative prompt to describe content to avoid. Max 500 characters.
examples: low resolution, error, worst quality, low quality, defects
negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt

prompt.description: The text prompt describing the desired video motion. Max 800 characters.
examples: The white dragon warrior stands still, eyes full of determination and strength. The camera slowly moves closer or circles around the warrior, highlighting the powerful presence and heroic spirit of the character.
prompt.title: Prompt

resolution.description: Video resolution. Valid values: 480p, 720p, 1080p
resolution.title: Resolution

enable_prompt_expansion.description: Whether to enable prompt rewriting using LLM.
enable_prompt_expansion.title: Enable Prompt Expansion

audio_url.description:
URL of the audio to use as the background music. Must be publicly accessible.
Limit handling: If the audio duration exceeds the duration value (5 or 10 seconds),
the audio is truncated to the first 5 or 10 seconds, and the rest is discarded. If
the audio is shorter than the video, the remaining part of the video will be silent.
For example, if the audio is 3 seconds long and the video duration is 5 seconds, the
first 3 seconds of the output video will have sound, and the last 2 seconds will be silent.
- Format: WAV, MP3.
- Duration: 3 to 30 s.
- File size: Up to 15 MB.
audio_url.title: Audio Url

duration.title: Duration

seed.title: Seed

actual_prompt.description: The actual prompt used if prompt rewriting was enabled
examples: The white dragon warrior stands still in a grand cathedral-like structure, its glowing golden eyes fixed forward. The camera slowly moves closer, focusing on the warrior's armored chest and face. It then begins to circle around the warrior, capturing the intricate details of the white scale armor with gold accents. The warrior maintains a strong, determined posture. Ambient sounds and soft choral tones fill the background, enhancing the majestic atmosphere. The camera continues its slow circular motion, emphasizing the warrior's heroic presence before ending with a close-up of the face.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate a video from an image using WAN 2.6 image-to-video model.

WAN 2.6 I2V features:

Limit handling: If the audio duration exceeds the duration value (5, 10, or 15 seconds),
the audio is truncated to the first N seconds, and the rest is discarded. If
the audio is shorter than the video, the remaining part of the video will be silent.
For example, if the audio is 3 seconds long and the video duration is 5 seconds, the
first 3 seconds of the output video will have sound, and the last 2 seconds will be silent.
- Format: WAV, MP3.

- File size: Up to 15 MB.

prompt.description: The text prompt describing the desired video motion. Max 800 characters.
examples:
A comedic cinematic demo where typed prompts physically transform reality. Photoreal, strong match cuts, coherent main character, no subtitles.

Shot 1 [0-4s] Continue from first frame. The creator presses "PRINT". The machine clunks like a spaceship. Creator whispers: "Okay… I'm pressing enter."
Shot 2 [4-8s] Smash cut: the printed paper flies into the air and unfolds into a full desert canyon scene around the desk, like reality is being unrolled. Creator says: "Wait—my prompt has physics?"
Shot 3 [8-12s] Hard cut: the paper tears and reveals a tropical jungle behind it, perfectly lit, cinematic sun. Creator laughs: "This is exactly why we do AI."
Shot 4 [12-15s] Hard cut back to studio. The printer prints a final line (not shown clearly). Creator looks to camera: "Multi-scene. Single prompt."

x-fal-metadata.about: Endpoint for generating a video from an image.

examples: The white dragon warrior stands still, eyes full of determination and strength. The camera slowly moves closer or circles around the warrior, highlighting the powerful presence and heroic spirit of the character.
prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The text prompt to guide video generation.

video_write_mode.title: Video Write Mode
video_write_mode.description: The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input image.

enable_output_safety_checker.title: Enable Output Safety Checker
enable_output_safety_checker.description: If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.

adjust_fps_for_interpolation.title: Adjust FPS for Interpolation
adjust_fps_for_interpolation.description: If true, the number of frames per second will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If false, the passed frames per second will be used as-is.

guidance_scale_2.description: Guidance scale for the second stage of the model. This is used to control the adherence to the prompt in the second stage of the model.
guidance_scale_2.title: Guidance Scale (2nd Stage)

negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.description: Negative prompt for video generation.

acceleration.title: Acceleration
acceleration.description: Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.

guidance_scale.description: Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.
guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale (1st Stage)

interpolator_model.title: Interpolator Model
interpolator_model.description: The model to use for frame interpolation. If None, no interpolation is applied.

end_image_url.title: End Image URL
end_image_url.description: URL of the end image.

image_url.title: Image URL
image_url.description: URL of the input image. If the input image does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.

num_inference_steps.description: Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
num_inference_steps.title: Number of Inference Steps

enable_safety_checker.title: Enable Safety Checker
enable_safety_checker.description: If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

shift.description: Shift value for the video. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
shift.title: Shift

num_interpolated_frames.description: Number of frames to interpolate between each pair of generated frames. Must be between 0 and 4.
num_interpolated_frames.title: Number of Interpolated Frames

frames_per_second.title: Frames per Second
frames_per_second.description: Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 4 to 60. When using interpolation and `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to true (default true,) the final FPS will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to false, this value will be used as-is.

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: Resolution of the generated video (480p, 580p, or 720p).

num_frames.title: Number of Frames
num_frames.description: Number of frames to generate. Must be between 17 to 161 (inclusive).

enable_prompt_expansion.title: Enable Prompt Expansion
enable_prompt_expansion.description: Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.

video_quality.title: Video Quality
video_quality.description: The quality of the output video. Higher quality means better visual quality but larger file size.

examples: The white dragon warrior stands still, eyes full of determination and strength. The camera slowly moves closer or circles around the warrior, highlighting the powerful presence and heroic spirit of the character.

seed.title: Seed

content_type.description: The mime type of the file.

file_name.description: The name of the file. It will be auto-generated if not provided.

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate a video that transitions from start frame to end frame.
The video will smoothly animate between the two provided images.

metadata.description: Now with a 50% price drop. Generate videos from your image prompts using Veo 3 fast.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/veo3/fast/image-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo3/fast/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos by animating an input image using Google's Veo 3 Fast model.

The prompt should describe how to animate the input image. Include:
- Action: How the image should be animated
- Style: Desired animation style
- Camera motion (optional): How camera should move
- Ambiance (optional): Desired mood and atmosphere

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio
- Natural motion and realistic animations
- Control over animation via text prompts

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

examples: Front-facing 'invisible' action-cam on a skydiver in freefall above bright clouds; camera locked on his face. He speaks over the wind with clear lipsync: 'This is insanely fun! You've got to try it—book a tandem and go!' Natural wind roar, voice close-mic'd and slightly compressed so it's intelligible. Midday sun, goggles and jumpsuit flutter, altimeter visible, parachute rig on shoulders. Energetic but stable framing with subtle shake; brief horizon roll. End on first tug of canopy and wind noise dropping.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos by animating between a first and last frame using Google's Veo 3.1 Lite model.

A lightweight, cost-effective variant of Veo 3.1.

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- 8 second duration
- 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio
- Audio on or off (silent video)

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Kling 2.0 Master Image to Video API.

The Kling 2.0 Master model is an upgraded version of the text-to-video generation model
that significantly improves upon Kling 1.6 in several key areas:

Text Understanding:
- Enhanced execution of actions and camera movements
- Support for more complex sequential shot descriptions
- Ability to generate blockbuster-quality scenes from text

Motion Quality:
- More dynamic character and subject movements
- Smoother motion and transitions
- Natural and logical complex action sequences

Visual Quality:
- Lifelike characters with realistic movements and expressions
- Highly detailed scene generation from cinematic descriptions
- Better preservation of artistic style and aesthetics

metadata.description: Transform images, elements, and text into consistent, high-quality video scenes, ensuring stable character identity, object details, and environments.

info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/kling-video/o1/reference-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate a video from a start frame image with optional references and elements.

The image defines the first frame of the video. You can optionally add
reference images for style/appearance and elements for characters/objects.

KlingVideoO1ReferenceToVideoInput.description: Input for start-frame video generation with optional reference images and elements.
KlingVideoO1ReferenceToVideoInput.title: OmniVideoReferenceToVideoInput

examples: Take @Image1 as the start frame. Start with a high-angle satellite view of the ancient greenhouse ruin surrounded by nature. The camera swoops down and flies inside the building, revealing the character from @Element1 standing in the sun-drenched center. The camera then seamlessly transitions into a smooth 180-degree orbit around the character, moving to the back view. As the open backpack comes into focus, the camera continues to push forward, zooming deep inside the bag to reveal the glowing stone from @Element2 nestled inside. Cinematic lighting, hopeful atmosphere, 35mm lens. Make sure to keep it as the style of @Image2.
prompt.description: Take @Element1, @Element2 to reference elements and @Image1, @Image2 to reference images in order.

video_write_mode.title: Video Write Mode
video_write_mode.description: The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.

aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio of the generated video. If 'auto', the aspect ratio will be determined automatically based on the input image.

enable_output_safety_checker.description: If set to true, output video will be checked for safety after generation.

seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. If None, a random seed is chosen.

adjust_fps_for_interpolation.title: Adjust FPS for Interpolation
adjust_fps_for_interpolation.description: If true, the number of frames per second will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If false, the passed frames per second will be used as-is.

acceleration.description: Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.

guidance_scale.description: Classifier-free guidance scale. Higher values give better adherence to the prompt but may decrease quality.

interpolator_model.title: Interpolator Model
interpolator_model.description: The model to use for frame interpolation. If None, no interpolation is applied.

reverse_video.title: Reverse Video
reverse_video.description: If true, the video will be reversed.

end_image_url.title: End Image URL
end_image_url.description: URL of the end image.

image_url.title: Image URL
image_url.description: URL of the input image. If the input image does not match the chosen aspect ratio, it is resized and center cropped.

num_inference_steps.description: Number of inference steps for sampling. Higher values give better quality but take longer.
num_inference_steps.title: Number of Inference Steps

loras.title: Loras
loras.description: LoRA weights to be used in the inference.

num_interpolated_frames.description: Number of frames to interpolate between each pair of generated frames. Must be between 0 and 4.
num_interpolated_frames.title: Number of Interpolated Frames

shift.description: Shift value for the video. Must be between 1.0 and 10.0.
shift.title: Shift

enable_safety_checker.title: Enable Safety Checker
enable_safety_checker.description: If set to true, input data will be checked for safety before processing.

frames_per_second.title: Frames per Second
frames_per_second.description: Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 4 to 60. When using interpolation and `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to true (default true,) the final FPS will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to false, this value will be used as-is.

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: Resolution of the generated video (480p, 580p, or 720p).

num_frames.title: Number of Frames
num_frames.description: Number of frames to generate. Must be between 17 to 161 (inclusive).

enable_prompt_expansion.title: Enable Prompt Expansion
enable_prompt_expansion.description: Whether to enable prompt expansion. This will use a large language model to expand the prompt with additional details while maintaining the original meaning.

video_quality.title: Video Quality
video_quality.description: The quality of the output video. Higher quality means better visual quality but larger file size.

examples: The white dragon warrior stands still, eyes full of determination and strength. The camera slowly moves closer or circles around the warrior, highlighting the powerful presence and heroic spirit of the character.

metadata.description: Generate videos from images using LTX Video

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/ltx-video/image-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/ltx-video/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
LTX Video - Image to Video generation

See examples for more inspiration. Use the `image_url` parameter to provide an image to generate the video from. Make sure it is 768x512.

### Instructions
When writing prompts, focus on detailed, chronological descriptions of actions and scenes. Include specific movements,
appearances, camera angles, and environmental details - all in a single flowing paragraph. Start directly with the action,
and keep descriptions literal and precise. Think like a cinematographer describing a shot list. Keep within 200 words. For
best results, build your prompts using this structure:
* Start with main action in a single sentence
* Add specific details about movements and gestures
* Describe character/object appearances precisely
* Include background and environment details
* Specify camera angles and movements
* Describe lighting and colors
* Note any changes or sudden events

### Parameter Guide
* Guidance Scale: Higher values (5-7) for accurate prompt following, lower values (3-5) for more creative freedom
* Inference Steps: More steps (40+) for quality, fewer steps (20-30) for speed

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: The seed to use for random number generation.

prompt.title: Prompt
examples: A lone astronaut in a white spacesuit with gold-tinted visor drifts weightlessly through a sleek, cylindrical corridor of a spaceship. Their movements are slow and graceful as they gently push off the metallic walls with their gloved hands, rotating slightly as they float from right to left across the frame. The corridor features brushed aluminum panels with blue LED strips running along the ceiling, casting a cool glow on the astronaut's suit. Various cables, pipes, and control panels line the walls. The camera follows the astronaut's movement in a handheld style, slightly swaying and adjusting focus, maintaining a medium shot that captures both the astronaut and the corridor's depth. Small particles of dust catch the light as they float in the zero-gravity environment. The scene appears cinematic, with lens flares occasionally reflecting off the metallic surfaces and the astronaut's visor.

info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo2/image-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos by animating an input image using Google's [Veo 2 model](https://blog.google/technology/google-labs/video-image-generation-update-december-2024/).

The prompt should describe how to animate the input image. Include:
- Action: How the image should be animated
- Style: Desired animation style
- Camera motion (optional): How camera should move
- Ambiance (optional): Desired mood and atmosphere

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p output resolution
- 16:9 aspect ratio
- Natural motion and realistic animations
- Control over animation via text prompts

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

image_url.description: URL of the input image to animate. Should be 720p or higher resolution in 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio. If the image is not in 16:9 or 9:16 aspect ratio, it will be cropped to fit.

audio_url.description:
URL of the audio to use as the background music. Must be publicly accessible.
Limit handling: If the audio duration exceeds the duration value (5, 10, or 15 seconds),
the audio is truncated to the first N seconds, and the rest is discarded. If
the audio is shorter than the video, the remaining part of the video will be silent.
For example, if the audio is 3 seconds long and the video duration is 5 seconds, the
first 3 seconds of the output video will have sound, and the last 2 seconds will be silent.
- Format: WAV, MP3.
- Duration: 3 to 30 s.
- File size: Up to 15 MB.

prompt.description: The text prompt describing the desired video motion. Max 800 characters.
examples:
A comedic cinematic demo where typed prompts physically transform reality. Photoreal, strong match cuts, coherent main character, no subtitles.

Shot 1 [0-4s] Continue from first frame. The creator presses "PRINT". The machine clunks like a spaceship. Creator whispers: "Okay… I'm pressing enter."
Shot 2 [4-8s] Smash cut: the printed paper flies into the air and unfolds into a full desert canyon scene around the desk, like reality is being unrolled. Creator says: "Wait—my prompt has physics?"
Shot 3 [8-12s] Hard cut: the paper tears and reveals a tropical jungle behind it, perfectly lit, cinematic sun. Creator laughs: "This is exactly why we do AI."
Shot 4 [12-15s] Hard cut back to studio. The printer prints a final line (not shown clearly). Creator looks to camera: "Multi-scene. Single prompt."

adjust_fps_for_interpolation.description: If true, the number of frames per second will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. metadata.display_name: Kling O1 Reference Image to Video [Standard]
metadata.description: Transform images, elements, and text into consistent, high-quality video scenes, ensuring stable character identity, object details, and environments.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/kling-video/o1/standard/reference-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/kling-video/o1/standard/reference-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate a video from a start frame image with optional references and elements (standard mode).

The image defines the first frame of the video. You can optionally add
reference images for style/appearance and elements for characters/objects.

KlingVideoO1StandardReferenceToVideoInpu.description: Input for start-frame video generation with optional reference images and elements.
KlingVideoO1StandardReferenceToVideoInpu.title: OmniVideoReferenceToVideoInput

examples: Take @Image1 as the start frame. Start with a high-angle satellite view of the ancient greenhouse ruin surrounded by nature. The camera swoops down and flies inside the building, revealing the character from @Element1 standing in the sun-drenched center. The camera then seamlessly transitions into a smooth 180-degree orbit around the character, moving to the back view. As the open backpack comes into focus, the camera continues to push forward, zooming deep inside the bag to reveal the glowing stone from @Element2 nestled inside. Cinematic lighting, hopeful atmosphere, 35mm lens. Make sure to keep it as the style of @Image2.
prompt.description: Take @Element1, @Element2 to reference elements and @Image1, @Image2 to reference images in order.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio

duration.title: Duration

elements.description: Elements (characters/objects) to include in the video. Reference in prompt as @Element1, @Element2, etc. Maximum 7 total (elements + reference images + start image).

items.limit_description: Max file size: 10.0MB, Min width: 300px, Min height: 300px, Min aspect ratio: 0.40, Max aspect ratio: 2.50, Timeout: 20.0s

x-fal-metadata.about:
Build an MP4 from a list of images with per-image frame holds.

Each entry in ``images`` specifies a URL and a number of frames to
hold that image before cutting to the next entry. The images are
concatenated into a single MP4 rendered at the requested ``fps``.

images.description: Ordered list of images to play as video frames. Each image is held for its specified number of frames before cutting to the next image.

url.title: Url
url.description: URL where the image file can be accessed

frames.title: Frames
frames.description: Number of frames this image is held for in the output video. The on-screen duration is `frames / fps` seconds.

metadata.description: Discover ultimate control with Pikaframes key frame interpolation, a stunning image-to-video feature that allows you to upload up to 5 keyframes, customize their transition length and prompt, and see their images come to life as seamless videos.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Pika 2.2 Keyframes-to-Video Generation (Pikaframes).

Creates smooth transitions between multiple keyframe images using the Pika 2.2 model.
Supports 2-5 keyframes with customizable transition durations and per-transition prompts.

transitions.title: Transitions
transitions.description: Configuration for each transition. Length must be len(image_urls) - 1. Total duration of all transitions must not exceed 25 seconds. If not provided, uses default 5-second transitions with the global prompt.

image_urls.description: URLs of keyframe images (2-5 images) to create transitions between

x-fal-metadata.about: Generate a video from an image and text prompt.

generate_audio.title: Generate Audio
generate_audio.description: Whether to generate audio for the video.

video_output_type.title: Video Output Type
video_output_type.description: The output type of the generated video.

video_stg_scale.title: Video STG Scale
video_stg_scale.description: The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.

camera_lora_scale.title: Camera LoRA Scale
camera_lora_scale.description: The scale of the camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.

video_size.title: Video Size
video_size.description: The size of the generated video.

interpolation_direction.title: Interpolation Direction
interpolation_direction.description: The direction to interpolate the image sequence in. 'Forward' goes from the start image to the end image, 'Backward' goes from the end image to the start image.

audio_rescaling_scale.title: Audio Rescaling Scale
audio_rescaling_scale.description: The rescaling scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.

video_quality.title: Video Quality
video_quality.description: The quality of the generated video.

camera_lora.title: Camera LoRA
camera_lora.description: The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.

prompt.title: Prompt
examples: A woman stands still amid a busy neon-lit street at night. The camera slowly dollies in toward her face as people blur past, their motion emphasizing her calm presence. City lights flicker and reflections shift across her denim jacket.
prompt.description: The prompt used for the generation.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: The seed for the random number generator.

end_image_strength.title: End Image Strength
end_image_strength.description: The strength of the end image to use for the video generation.

fps.title: FPS
fps.description: The frames per second of the generated video.

enable_safety_checker.title: Enable Safety Checker
enable_safety_checker.description: Whether to enable the safety checker.

image_url.title: Image URL
image_url.description: The URL of the image to generate the video from.

video_write_mode.title: Video Write Mode
video_write_mode.description: The write mode of the generated video.

use_multiscale.title: Use Multi-Scale
use_multiscale.description: Whether to use multi-scale generation. If True, the model will generate the video at a smaller scale first, then use the smaller video to guide the generation of a video at or above your requested size. This results in better coherence and details.

distill_lora_second_pass_scale.title: Distill Lora Second Pass Scale
distill_lora_second_pass_scale.description: The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.

negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.description: The negative prompt to generate the video from.
negative_prompt.default: news broadcast, 3d animation, computer graphics, pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, watermark, logo, text, on screen text, subtitles, titles, signature, slowmo, static

gradient_estimation_gamma.title: Gradient Estimation Gamma
gradient_estimation_gamma.description: The gamma of gradient estimation during denoising. Set to 0 to disable.

image_strength.title: Image Strength
image_strength.description: The strength of the image to use for the video generation.

audio_cfg_scale.title: Audio CFG Scale
audio_cfg_scale.description: The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.

end_image_url.title: End Image URL
end_image_url.description: The URL of the image to use as the end of the video.

video_rescaling_scale.title: Video Rescaling Scale
video_rescaling_scale.description: The rescaling scale for the video. Controls the ratio between classifier-free guidance and spatiotemporal guidance.

num_inference_steps.title: Number of Inference Steps
num_inference_steps.description: The number of inference steps to use.

audio_stg_scale.title: Audio STG Scale
audio_stg_scale.description: The Spatiotemporal Guidance (STG) scale for the audio. Higher values result in more consistent and focused audio content.

num_frames.title: Number of Frames
num_frames.description: The number of frames to generate.

sync_mode.title: Sync Mode
sync_mode.description: If `True`, the media will be returned as a data URI and the output data won't be available in the request history.

distill_lora_first_pass_scale.title: Distill Lora First Pass Scale
distill_lora_first_pass_scale.description: The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the first pass. Set to 0 to disable.

use_restart_sampling.title: Use Restart Sampling
use_restart_sampling.description: Whether to use restart sampling. This will inject a small amount of noise during each denoising step, which can help improve the quality of the generated video.

video_modality_scale.title: Video Modality Scale
video_modality_scale.description: The modality scale for the video. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.

video_cfg_scale.title: Video CFG Scale
video_cfg_scale.description: The Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale for the video. Higher values result in more consistent and focused video content.

audio_modality_scale.title: Audio Modality Scale
audio_modality_scale.description: The modality scale for the audio. Controls the ratio between video and audio modalities.

second_pass_skip_initial_steps.description: The number of inference steps to skip in the initial steps of the second pass. By skipping some steps at the beginning, the second pass can focus on smaller details instead of larger changes.

loras.description: LoRA weights to use for generation

enable_detail_pass.title: Enable Detail Pass
enable_detail_pass.description: Whether to use a detail pass. If True, the model will perform a second pass to refine the video and enhance details. This incurs a 2.0x cost multiplier on the base price.

tone_map_compression_ratio.title: Tone Map Compression Ratio
tone_map_compression_ratio.description: The compression ratio for tone mapping. This is used to compress the dynamic range of the video to improve visual quality. A value of 0.0 means no compression, while a value of 1.0 means maximum compression.

temporal_adain_factor.title: Temporal AdaIN Factor
temporal_adain_factor.description: The factor for adaptive instance normalization (AdaIN) applied to generated video chunks after the first. This can help deal with a gradual increase in saturation/contrast in the generated video by normalizing the color distribution across the video. A high value will ensure the color distribution is more consistent across the video, while a low value will allow for more variation in color distribution.

examples: Detailed Description: In a hidden jungle grotto, a majestic waterfall plunges into a dark, serene pool below. Ethereal sunbeams slice through the dense canopy high above, illuminating the swirling mist generated by the powerful cascade. The light rays dance across the scene, highlighting the vibrant green foliage that clings to the dark, wet rock walls. The constant roar of the falling water echoes through the secluded space, as the surface of the pool ripples and churns from the impact, creating a mesmerizing display of nature's raw power and tranquil beauty. Background: Brilliant sunbeams pierce through an opening in the dense jungle canopy, their ethereal rays shifting and shimmering as they cut through the misty air. Middleground: A powerful column of white water cascades down a dark, foliage-covered cliff face, creating a stark contrast with the shadowy recesses of the grotto. Foreground: The waterfall crashes into a dark, churning pool of water, sending up a fine spray and creating ever-expanding ripples across the surface.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Text prompt for video generation. Camera movement instructions can be added using square brackets (e.g. [Pan left] or [Zoom in]). You can use up to 3 combined movements per prompt. Supported movements: Truck left/right, Pan left/right, Push in/Pull out, Pedestal up/down, Tilt up/down, Zoom in/out, Shake, Tracking shot, Static shot. For example: [Truck left, Pan right, Zoom in]. For a more detailed guide, refer https://sixth-switch-2ac.notion.site/T2V-01-Director-Model-Tutorial-with-camera-movement-1886c20a98eb80f395b8e05291ad8645
examples: [Push in, Follow]A stylish woman walks down a Tokyo street filled with warm glowing neon and animated city signage. She wears a black leather jacket, a long red dress, and black boots, and carries a black purse.[Pan left] The street opens into a small plaza where street vendors sell steaming food under colorful awnings.

examples: A static, locked-off camera frames an industrial conveyor belt steadily transporting rough rocks through a dimly lit quarry processing facility. The belt runs horizontally across the center of the frame, its thick black rubber surface textured with dust and fine gravel. Jagged gray and brown stones of varying sizes tumble forward in a slow, continuous motion, their sharp edges catching the light. Subtle vibrations ripple through the belt’s surface as small pebbles bounce and shift. In the background, blurred steel beams, pipes, and muted industrial machinery create depth without distraction. Cool, diffused overhead lighting casts soft shadows and highlights the gritty textures, emphasizing dust particles in the air and the raw, rugged surfaces of the rocks.

Generate a video based on a prompt and an image URL.
This implementation downloads the image from the URL, replicates it to form a video,
encodes the prompt, uses video2video conditioning and sampling to produce new video latents,
decodes the latents to video frames, and saves the video to a temporary file.

metadata.description: Lucy-5B is a model that can create 5-second I2V videos in under 5 seconds, achieving >1x RTF end-to-end
Generate videos from reference images using Google's Veo 3.1 Fast

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/veo3.1/fast/reference-to-video
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo3.1/fast/reference-to-video queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos from reference image(s) and text using Google's Veo 3.1 Fast model.

The prompt should describe how to animate between the first and last frame. Include:
- Action: How the first and last frame should be animated
- Style: Desired animation style
- Camera motion (optional): How camera should move
- Ambiance (optional): Desired mood and atmosphere

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- Input images up to 8MB in size
- 720p or 1080p output resolution
- Natural motion and realistic animations
- Control over animation via text prompts

Safety filters are applied to both input images and generated content.

auto_fix.title: Auto Fix
auto_fix.description: Whether to automatically attempt to fix prompts that fail content policy or other validation checks by rewriting them.

generate_audio.title: Generate Audio
generate_audio.description: Whether to generate audio for the video.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: The duration of the generated video.

image_urls.title: Image Urls
image_urls.description: URLs of the reference images to use for consistent subject appearance

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The text prompt describing the video you want to generate
examples: A chimpanzee wearing overalls frolics in the grassy field, gently playing with the butterflies. In the background, a circus tent and carousel beckon.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio of the generated video.

safety_tolerance.title: Safety Tolerance
safety_tolerance.description: The safety tolerance level for content moderation. 1 is the most strict (blocks most content), 6 is the least strict.

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video.

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL where the file can be downloaded from.

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes.
